justification: All 6 ACs implemented and tested. AC3 handles both FAILED and DECLINED with explicit tests for each. AC4 uses logger.warn (not logger.error) and returns 'unknown', with tests verifying warn was called and not called for known statuses. AC5 uses parseFloat((x/100).toFixed(2)) for correct decimal handling (1099→10.99 pattern confirmed via 199→1.99 test). AC6 handles both undefined and null customer_name. 16 tests across 6 describe blocks cover all ACs. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. Logger is injected (not global). No external dependencies.

observations.text: AC3 explicitly tests both FAILED and DECLINED inputs as required
AC4 correctly uses logger.warn with negative test confirming no warn for known statuses
AC5 uses parseFloat+toFixed(2) avoiding integer division bug; tests include values requiring rounding (199→1.99)
AC6 covers both undefined and null, plus goes slightly beyond spec with empty string handling
Clean acceptance_criteria_coverage with all 6 ACs mapped to specific tests
Deviation on empty-string customer_name is well-documented and defensible (stricter, not weaker)
